diagnoses.name: Displaced comminuted fracture of the shaft of the right tibia
diagnoses.evidence_span: She sustained a displaced comminuted fracture of the shaft of the right tibia.

diagnoses.name: Epilepsy
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient has a significant past medical history including epilepsy, which is currently unspecified and not intractable without documented status epilepticus.

diagnoses.name: Legal blindness
diagnoses.evidence_span: She also carries a diagnosis of legal blindness as defined in the USA, which likely contributed to her fall risk.

diagnoses.name: Mental disorder due to a known physiological condition
diagnoses.evidence_span: Additionally, she has an unspecified mental disorder due to a known physiological condition, for which she has been on psychiatric medications.

diagnoses.name: Fall on the same level
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient is a resident of a residential care facility who presented to the emergency department after a fall on the same level at her place of residence.

procedures.name: Operative reposition of the right tibia with open approach and intramedullary internal fixation device placement
procedures.evidence_span: The patient underwent operative reposition of the right tibia with open approach and intramedullary internal fixation device placement on hospital day 1.

medications.name: Cefazolin
medications.evidence_span: She received perioperative antibiotic prophylaxis with cefazolin.

medications.name: Oxycodone immediate release
medications.evidence_span: pain was managed with a combination of oxycodone immediate release, morphine sulfate IV as needed, and ketorolac.

medications.name: Morphine sulfate
medications.evidence_span: pain was managed with a combination of oxycodone immediate release, morphine sulfate IV as needed, and ketorolac.

medications.name: Ketorolac
medications.evidence_span: pain was managed with a combination of oxycodone immediate release, morphine sulfate IV as needed, and ketorolac.

medications.name: Enoxaparin
medications.evidence_span: Prophylactic enoxaparin was administered for thromboembolism prevention.

medications.name: Trazodone
medications.evidence_span: her trazodone, citalopram, and quetiapine were continued and titrated during the admission.

medications.name: Citalopram
medications.evidence_span: her trazodone, citalopram, and quetiapine were continued and titrated during the admission.

medications.name: Quetiapine fumarate
medications.evidence_span: her trazodone, citalopram, and quetiapine were continued and titrated during the admission.

medications.name: Divalproex
medications.evidence_span: Divalproex was maintained for seizure management.

medications.name: Docusate sodium
medications.evidence_span: The patient received appropriate bowel regimen including docusate sodium to prevent narcotic-induced constipation.

medications.name: Ondansetron
medications.evidence_span: Antiemetics with ondansetron were used as needed.

medications.name: Dextrose and normal saline
medications.evidence_span: IV fluid support with dextrose and normal saline was provided, along with potassium supplementation.

medications.name: Potassium supplementation

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.evidence_span: Acetaminophen was administered for additional analgesic coverage.

chief_complaint: Right leg pain and swelling following a fall.
